paper_title: Grip and detachment of locusts on inverted sandpaper substrates
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/02_Grip and detachment of locusts on inverted sandpaper substrates.pdf
page_numbering_note: File PDF page 1 is a download cover; printed article pages 1-10 correspond to file PDF pages 2-11.
sections_checked: 3.1 Critical angle of detachment and grasping force
3.2 Observation on claw tips and sandpaper texture
4.1 Adjusting body position on different slopes
4.3 Model of contact between locusts and sloping substrates when detachment takes place
4.4 Model of contact between claws and particles when detachment takes place

equations_checked.notes: Retained as E1; all three squared force components and the square root were visually checked.
Retained as E1; arctan(F_N^d/F_L^d) and the stated angle interval were visually checked.
Retained within E4; normal gravity component and pi-theta angle were visually checked.
Retained within E4; factor of two, sin(beta), and gravity term were visually checked.
Retained within E4; normal force equals one half of the normal gravity component at detachment.
Retained as E2; cosine and negative sine terms were visually checked.
Retained as E2; limiting static-friction expression and both positive terms were visually checked.
Retained as E3; numerator f*tan(alpha)+1 and denominator f-tan(alpha) were visually checked. The adjacent prose states that stable grip uses the greater-than-or-equal inequality.
Verified but omitted as the theta=pi ceiling special case of the retained two-support model.

tables_checked.notes: All seven grit grades, mean particle diameters, and roughness values were checked; ranges retained in the parameter table.
All seven F_L^d/F_N^d mean and standard-deviation values were checked. Only six p-values are printed, so the adjacent-comparison mapping remains uncertain and was not used quantitatively.

figures_checked.reason: Apparatus layout is adequately summarized by the two independent three-axis sensors, rotating platform, and 215 fps video description.
Irreplaceable experimental chain linking particle size, critical angle, lateral-force evolution, force ratio, and support angle. Crop visually verified with all axes, units, labels, and legends.
Direct morphology evidence for finite claw-tip size versus three sandpaper scales and local contact angle. Crop visually verified with scale bars, axes, units, and labels.
Body posture and claw-plus-pad contact observations can be retained accurately in text; the full biological montage is not required for downstream modeling.
Irreplaceable combined definition of whole-body axes/support angle and local claw-particle r/R/alpha force geometry. Crop visually verified with all four panels and labels.
The alpha-friction-force-ratio relationship is completely represented by retained Eq. (6), its domain, and the P600 numerical comparison.

omitted_sections: Most general insect-attachment background in the Introduction
Animal housing, sensor fabrication, and ordinary apparatus details not affecting interpretation
Detailed leg-angle ranges not needed for the passive engineering model

uncertain_items.detail: Figure 2 states n=112 and N=11 locusts, while the method states 11 individuals and seven repeats. The paper does not explain how 112 observations were formed.
Seven force-ratio groups are printed but only six p-values are shown, apparently for adjacent comparisons; the exact mapping is not explicitly labeled.
The P1200 critical angle is reported with a standard deviation of 5.0 degrees in the results continuation and 4.9 degrees in Section 4.4.

Fig. 3 shows representative local alpha values, while later numerical examples use different alpha values for the same grit families. Alpha cannot be treated as a unique function of mean particle diameter.

known_warnings: Eq. (6) is meaningful as a stable threshold only for f > tan(alpha) and positive contact normal force.
Measured forces are active multi-leg whole-animal reactions and cannot be used as isolated single-spine capacities.
The local model assumes spherical tips and particles, one contact, no material damage, and no compliant pad contribution.
The model explicitly fails to explain P1000 and P1200; the authors infer a roughness window that is poor for both claw interlocking and pad adhesion.
All sandpaper thresholds and force values require remeasurement or recalculation for fired-clay brick and engineered spines.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows, and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains both a local contact model and a whole-body detachment validation model.
